steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice best explains how the Jules Falk Stradivarius got its name. The relevant note is: “Many of the 500 or so that exist today are named for a previous owner” and “The Jules Falk Stradivarius is named for Jules Falk, an American violinist.” The answer must connect the naming convention (former owner) to this specific violin.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Jules Falk was an American violinist.: States a fact about Falk but gives no explanation of how the violin got its name. ✗
  • Choice B — Of the 1,000 or so violins Antonio Stradivari made, only about 500 exist today.: Discusses survival counts, not the naming convention at all. ✗
  • Choice C — Designed by Antonio Stradivari, Stradivarius violins in the Jules Falk are renowned for their quality.: Garbled phrasing and about sound quality, not naming. ✗
  • Choice D — The Jules Falk Stradivarius is named after its former owner, Jules Falk.: Directly applies the naming convention (former owner) to explain this violin's name. ✓
That is the correct answer.
